authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 141, Entry of Merchandise
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. customs workflow rule for entry documentation, invoice content, and tariff/value reporting requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Entry-document intake and evidence sufficiency review

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 152, Classification and Appraisement of Merchandise
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary CBP regulation for tariff-classification framework and for valuation method ordering, transaction value, fallback methods, and unacceptable bases.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 163, Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary recordkeeping and auditability regulation for maintaining, producing, and examining customs records that support entries and post-entry review.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 177, Administrative Rulings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative source for ruling requests, situations where no ruling will be issued, and the effect of ruling letters on customs transactions.

authoritative_sources.title: Tariff Classification
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP regulator guidance that translates classification principles into importer and broker review expectations for practical customs workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Classification evidence review and terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Customs Value
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP regulator guidance describing valuation methods, transaction value concepts, and common adjustment issues for import review work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Valuation evidence review and method selection

authoritative_sources.title: Reconciliation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative CBP post-entry workflow reference for revising undeterminable value and certain classification elements through reconciliation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Post-entry review, discrepancy handling, and corrective follow-up

authoritative_sources.title: Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. tariff schedule used to determine headings, subheadings, and duty rates for classification review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Tariff-position review and duty implication assessment

authoritative_sources.title: WCO Harmonized System Explanatory Notes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized international classification interpretation aid used to constrain heading scope and normalize customs terminology.

authoritative_sources.title: WTO Customs Valuation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized multilateral valuation framework reinforcing that customs valuation must be fair, uniform, and not based on arbitrary or fictitious values.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Valuation method constraints and failure-mode design

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the entry, commodity, party, and tenant authority context needed before any classification or valuation assessment can be trusted.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Classification evidence assessment
workflow_stages.description: Review product identity, HTSUS candidates, prior rulings, and explanatory sources to determine whether the record supports a classification finding.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Valuation evidence assessment
workflow_stages.description: Review transaction value, required additions or deductions, related-party facts, and method-order constraints for valuation sufficiency.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Authority conflict and post-entry follow-up
workflow_stages.description: Determine when rulings, protests, reconciliation, CF28/CF29 follow-up, or tenant escalation rules change the path or prevent closure.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Findings package and escalation routing
workflow_stages.description: Produce assessment records, follow-up notes, and authority-safe escalation packages without claiming final legal or licensed signoff.

artifacts: Product specifications, BOMs, technical literature, samples, and photos
HTSUS candidates, GRI analysis notes, and prior CBP ruling references
Purchase orders, contracts, payment records, and Incoterms evidence
Assists schedules, royalty or license agreements, and related-party fact sets
CBP Form 7501 data, entry summaries, reconciliation flags, and post-summary correction records
CF28 or CF29 requests, broker workpapers, communication logs, and remediation notes
Finance ledger tie-outs, adjustment worksheets, and approval history
systems_of_record: Broker operating system or customs entry platform
Document management and broker workpaper repository
Accounts payable, treasury, or payment-support system
Ruling library, protest tracker, and case-management tools
Fraud, AML, and compliance escalation systems

allowed: Determine whether available evidence supports, fails, or leaves unclear a proposed classification or valuation position.
Produce findings summaries, evidence checklists, remediation notes, and escalation packets tied to named records.
Identify when reconciliation, ruling review, or post-entry follow-up is the correct next step.
escalate: A CBP ruling, protest, court posture, or tenant legal policy materially changes the answer.
Undervaluation, related-party pricing irregularity, or suspicious transaction facts indicate fraud, AML, or legal risk.
Ledger, invoice, and entry-summary records conflict and require reconciliation or human resolution.
refuse: The request asks for final legal signoff, binding classification authority, or formal valuation approval outside the lane.
Required product facts, valuation records, or tenant authority context are missing and would change the result.
The request requires a fabricated product description, valuation element, or unsupported override of documented policy.

domain_failure_modes: Using commercial shorthand or generic product labels that are too weak to support tariff classification.
Accepting transaction value without proving assists, royalties, commissions, or related-party effects.
Treating a prior ruling as universally controlling when the facts or merchandise are materially different.
Closing a review despite contradictions between entry data, commercial records, and finance tie-outs.
Conflating operational review support with legal interpretation, final broker signoff, or fraud adjudication.
Ignoring reconciliation or post-entry correction paths when value or classification elements were indeterminable at filing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Inspection or review evidence is missing or inconclusive
Finding changes based on formal interpretation or official authority
Stakeholder asks for pass/fail signoff beyond documented lane authority
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when prerequisites are missing
